title: Permission error when trying to access a volume defined in an image with custom user and --userns=auto enabled
body:
### Issue Description

When executing a container with `--userns=auto` and using an image including a volume mount (e.g. `VOLUME["/data"]`) and a specific user (e.g. redis with uid 999) I get a permission error when trying to access the volume. The error can be avoided by either dropping `--userns=auto` or by adding `--volume=my-vol:/data:U`. 

It seems that on container startup the ownership of directory `~/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes/[my-vol]/_data` is changed to the container user, if the uid:gid is not part of the current user namespace. This process seems to have an error, because the uid:gid is not calculated correctly: Instead of having an uid:guid from the current user namespace I get the uid:gid without any user namespace. 


### Steps to reproduce the issue

A concrete example just focusing on the uid for simplicity:
```
cat /etc/subuid
podman:1000000:10000000
```

Start a container to reserve some subuids
```
podman run -d --rm --userns=auto:size=100000 ubi8 sleep 1800
```

Start a redis container using uid=999 as redis user and having a VOLUME["/data"] definition.
```
podman run -it --rm --userns=auto:size=100000 --entrypoint=bash docker.io/redis -c " touch /data/test; ls -la /data; sleep 10"
total 12
drwxr-xr-x. 2 nobody nogroup 4096 Nov 12 02:37 .
dr-xr-xr-x. 1 root   root    4096 Nov 30 12:55 ..
touch: cannot touch '/data/test': Permission denied
```

And checking the volume on the host system, it's clear that the _data folder has the wrong ownership, compared to the parent which has the right ownership:
```
ls -la /home/podman/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes/*/_data/
total 8
drwxr-xr-x. 2 1000998 1000998 4096 Nov 12 03:37 .
drwx------. 3 1100000 1100000 4096 Nov 30 13:55 ..
```

The _data folder has the same ownership as when the container was not created with `--userns=auto:size=100000`, which of course cannot work.
```
podman run -it --rm --entrypoint=bash docker.io/redis -c "touch /data/test; ls -la /data; sleep 5"
total 12
drwxr-xr-x. 2 redis redis 4096 Nov 30 12:55 .
dr-xr-xr-x. 1 root  root  4096 Nov 30 12:55 ..
-rw-r--r--. 1 root  root     0 Nov 30 12:55 test
```
```
ls -la /home/podman/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes/*/_data/
total 8
drwxr-xr-x. 2 1000998 1000998 4096 Nov 30 13:55 .
drwx------. 3 podman  podman  4096 Nov 30 13:55 ..
-rw-r--r--. 1 podman  podman     0 Nov 30 13:55 test
```

### Describe the results you received

The uid of the _data folder is 1000998 (nobody) instead of 1100000 (root). The uid will be set correctly if adding `--volume=my-vol:/data:U`, but this comes with a performance impact for big volumes and dropping `--userns=auto:size=100000` comes with a security impact.


### Describe the results you expected

I expect that the _data folder has the same (correct) ownership as his parent. More I expect that the root user of the container is owning the volume and not some "random" uid which is not part of the user namespace of the container.

For my concrete example I expect:
```
ls -la /home/podman/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes/*/_data/
total 8
drwxr-xr-x. 2 1100000 1100000 4096 Nov 30 13:56 .
drwx------. 3 1100000 1100000 4096 Nov 30 13:56 ..
-rw-r--r--. 1 1100000 1100000    0 Nov 30 13:56 test
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

I have the error on alma linux 9 with podman 5.2.2 with selinux enabled. I didn't have the error before on ubuntu 24.04 without selinux enabled and with podman ~4.9.

comments.body: it is already fixed upstream with 432325236b7d195045edb9a4cc47be00f71d407f
